    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h", "symbol": "GrpcMuxSotw"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h", "symbol": "GrpcMuxDelta"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/subscription_state.h", "symbol": "SubscriptionState"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/subscription_state.h", "symbol": "BaseSubscriptionState"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h", "symbol": "SotwSubscriptionState"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h", "symbol": "SotwSubscriptionStateFactory"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h", "symbol": "DeltaSubscriptionState"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h", "symbol": "DeltaSubscriptionStateFactory"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h", "symbol": "WatchMap"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.h", "symbol": "DeltaSubscriptionState"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_context.h", "symbol": "GrpcMuxContext"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_stream_interface.h", "symbol": "GrpcStreamInterface"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_failover.h", "symbol": "GrpcMuxFailover"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/common/config/null_grpc_mux_impl.h", "symbol": "NullGrpcMuxImpl"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "envoy/config/grpc_mux.h", "symbol": "GrpcMux"}
  ],
  "text": "The Envoy xDS v3 transport protocol implementation consists of multiple components organized into legacy and unified architectures:\n\n## Core Architecture\n\nThe **GrpcMux interface** (envoy/config/grpc_mux.h) defines the abstract interface for managing gRPC subscriptions to xDS APIs. It supports multiplexing multiple resource types over a single gRPC stream for ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service).\n\n## Legacy Implementations\n\n**GrpcSubscriptionImpl** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_impl.h/.cc) is the primary adapter that bridges the xDS transport layer with Envoy's configuration subscription interface. It manages subscriptions for individual resource types and handles callbacks from the mux implementations. GrpcCollectionSubscriptionImpl extends this for collection resources.\n\n**GrpcMuxImpl** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.h/.cc) implements the State-of-the-World (SotW) xDS protocol. It manages the gRPC stream lifecycle, multiplexes multiple resource types, and uses DeltaSubscriptionState for tracking protocol state. This is the legacy SotW implementation.\n\n**NewGrpcMuxImpl** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.h/.cc) implements the Delta xDS protocol variant, which sends only resource changes rather than the complete state. It manages subscriptions per resource type and tracks updates more efficiently for large datasets.\n\n**GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory** and **DeltaGrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.h/.cc) are factory classes that select between SotW and Delta modes based on configuration. They instantiate the appropriate mux implementation and wrap it with GrpcSubscriptionImpl.\n\n## Unified XdsMux Implementation\n\nThe xds_mux subdirectory contains a unified, templated implementation replacing the legacy split:\n\n**GrpcMuxImpl template** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h/.cc) is a template class parameterized on subscription state type (S), state factory (F), and request/response message types. This single implementation supports both SotW and Delta through template specialization.\n\n**GrpcMuxSotw** and **GrpcMuxDelta** are concrete template instantiations:\n- GrpcMuxSotw uses SotwSubscriptionState with DiscoveryRequest/DiscoveryResponse\n- GrpcMuxDelta uses DeltaSubscriptionState with DeltaDiscoveryRequest/DeltaDiscoveryResponse\n\n**SubscriptionState hierarchy** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/subscription_state.h):\n- SubscriptionState is the base marker class\n- BaseSubscriptionState<RS, RQ> is the template base for protocol state tracking\n- **SotwSubscriptionState** (sotw_subscription_state.h/.cc) tracks full state updates per resource type\n- **DeltaSubscriptionState** (delta_subscription_state.h/.cc) tracks incremental updates with resource versioning\n- Both use **SotwSubscriptionStateFactory** and **DeltaSubscriptionStateFactory** respectively\n\n## Supporting Infrastructure\n\n**WatchMap** (watch_map.h/.cc) maintains the watches-to-subscription mapping, delivering updates to all interested watchers for a given resource type.\n\n**GrpcMuxContext** (grpc_mux_context.h) is a struct containing all parameters needed to create GrpcMux objects: async client, dispatcher, service method descriptor, local info, rate limiting settings, and resource caches.\n\n**GrpcStreamInterface** (grpc_stream_interface.h) defines the low-level gRPC stream interface for sending/receiving xDS messages.\n\n**GrpcMuxFailover** (grpc_mux_failover.h) implements failover between primary and failover xDS config sources.\n\n**NullGrpcMuxImpl** (source/common/config/null_grpc_mux_impl.h) is a no-op implementation used when ADS is not configured.\n\nThe legacy delta_subscription_state.h/.cc in the parent directory provides the original Delta implementation used by NewGrpcMuxImpl before the unified template was introduced.\n\nThe migration from legacy (GrpcMuxImpl/NewGrpcMuxImpl + separate state classes) to unified (templated GrpcMuxImpl with GrpcMuxSotw/GrpcMuxDelta) is controlled by the runtime feature \"envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux\" in grpc_subscription_factory.cc."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

**Sourcegraph Repositories:** `github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2`, `github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367`, `github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6`, `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e`

# Envoy xDS v3 Transport Protocol Implementation

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that implement the xDS v3 transport protocol. Identify: the GrpcSubscriptionImpl adapter, the SotW (State of the World) GrpcMuxImpl, the Delta xDS NewGrpcMuxImpl, the GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory that selects between SotW and Delta modes, and the unified XdsMux template that supports both variants.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all C++ source files implementing the xDS v3 transport protocol in Envoy. Let me start by searching for the key components you mentioned.
Let me search for more specific files related to the unified XdsMux implementation:
Let me search for additional related files to ensure completeness:
Let me search for a few more key files to ensure completeness:
Let me search for the GrpcMux interface and other header files:
Let me read the key header files to ensure completeness:
Let me read those files without the limit parameter:
Now let me compile all the findings into the answer file:

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the xDS v3 transport protocol implementation in Envoy. Here's what I found:

## Summary

I've identified **25 core C++ source files** implementing the xDS v3 transport protocol in Envoy, organized into two architectural approaches:

### **Legacy Implementations:**
1. **GrpcSubscriptionImpl** - The adapter that bridges xDS transport with Envoy's subscription interface
2. **GrpcMuxImpl** - SotW (State-of-the-World) protocol implementation
3. **NewGrpcMuxImpl** - Delta xDS protocol implementation
4. **GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory** - Factory that selects between SotW and Delta modes

### **Unified XdsMux Template:**
5. **GrpcMuxImpl template** (xds_mux/) - Single parameterized implementation supporting both variants
6. **GrpcMuxSotw** & **GrpcMuxDelta** - Concrete template instantiations
7. **SubscriptionState hierarchy** - SotwSubscriptionState and DeltaSubscriptionState for protocol state tracking
8. **WatchMap** - Manages resource watches and callbacks
9. **Supporting infrastructure** - GrpcMuxContext, GrpcStreamInterface, GrpcMuxFailover, etc.

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with:
- Complete file list with repository paths
- All key symbols and their locations
- A detailed narrative explaining the architecture and relationships between components
- Information about the runtime-controlled migration from legacy to unified implementation via the `envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux` feature flag

    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h", "symbol": "SotwSubscriptionStateFactory"},

    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h", "symbol": "WatchMap"},

    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "envoy/config/grpc_mux.h", "symbol": "GrpcMux"}
  ],
  "text": "The Envoy xDS v3 transport protocol implementation consists of multiple components organized into legacy and unified architectures:\n\n## Core Architecture\n\nThe **GrpcMux interface** (envoy/config/grpc_mux.h) defines the abstract interface for managing gRPC subscriptions to xDS APIs. It supports multiplexing multiple resource types over a single gRPC stream for ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service).\n\n## Legacy Implementations\n\n**GrpcSubscriptionImpl** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_impl.h/.cc) is the primary adapter that bridges the xDS transport layer with Envoy's configuration subscription interface. It manages subscriptions for individual resource types and handles callbacks from the mux implementations. GrpcCollectionSubscriptionImpl extends this for collection resources.\n\n**GrpcMuxImpl** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.h/.cc) implements the State-of-the-World (SotW) xDS protocol. It manages the gRPC stream lifecycle, multiplexes multiple resource types, and uses DeltaSubscriptionState for tracking protocol state. This is the legacy SotW implementation.\n\n**NewGrpcMuxImpl** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.h/.cc) implements the Delta xDS protocol variant, which sends only resource changes rather than the complete state. It manages subscriptions per resource type and tracks updates more efficiently for large datasets.\n\n**GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory** and **DeltaGrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.h/.cc) are factory classes that select between SotW and Delta modes based on configuration. They instantiate the appropriate mux implementation and wrap it with GrpcSubscriptionImpl.\n\n## Unified XdsMux Implementation\n\nThe xds_mux subdirectory contains a unified, templated implementation replacing the legacy split:\n\n**GrpcMuxImpl template** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h/.cc) is a template class parameterized on subscription state type (S), state factory (F), and request/response message types. This single implementation supports both SotW and Delta through template specialization.\n\n**GrpcMuxSotw** and **GrpcMuxDelta** are concrete template instantiations:\n- GrpcMuxSotw uses SotwSubscriptionState with DiscoveryRequest/DiscoveryResponse\n- GrpcMuxDelta uses DeltaSubscriptionState with DeltaDiscoveryRequest/DeltaDiscoveryResponse\n\n**SubscriptionState hierarchy** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/subscription_state.h):\n- SubscriptionState is the base marker class\n- BaseSubscriptionState<RS, RQ> is the template base for protocol state tracking\n- **SotwSubscriptionState** (sotw_subscription_state.h/.cc) tracks full state updates per resource type\n- **DeltaSubscriptionState** (delta_subscription_state.h/.cc) tracks incremental updates with resource versioning\n- Both use **SotwSubscriptionStateFactory** and **DeltaSubscriptionStateFactory** respectively\n\n## Supporting Infrastructure\n\n**WatchMap** (watch_map.h/.cc) maintains the watches-to-subscription mapping, delivering updates to all interested watchers for a given resource type.\n\n**GrpcMuxContext** (grpc_mux_context.h) is a struct containing all parameters needed to create GrpcMux objects: async client, dispatcher, service method descriptor, local info, rate limiting settings, and resource caches.\n\n**GrpcStreamInterface** (grpc_stream_interface.h) defines the low-level gRPC stream interface for sending/receiving xDS messages.\n\n**GrpcMuxFailover** (grpc_mux_failover.h) implements failover between primary and failover xDS config sources.\n\n**NullGrpcMuxImpl** (source/common/config/null_grpc_mux_impl.h) is a no-op implementation used when ADS is not configured.\n\nThe legacy delta_subscription_state.h/.cc in the parent directory provides the original Delta implementation used by NewGrpcMuxImpl before the unified template was introduced.\n\nThe migration from legacy (GrpcMuxImpl/NewGrpcMuxImpl + separate state classes) to unified (templated GrpcMuxImpl with GrpcMuxSotw/GrpcMuxDelta) is controlled by the runtime feature \"envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux\" in grpc_subscription_factory.cc."
}
